headline: Across all 20 agents the single dominant root cause is identical: prompts predate the Plan-21 shared rubric and inherit NONE of it (no severity ladder, no codebase-bar, no internal-consistency, no scope clause). Every good-fixture over-flag (compliance fp 1.00, data-integrity 1.00, security 1.00, test-quality 1.00, observability ~0.83-1.00, performance low-noise 1.00, env-parity 0.67) is a codebase-bar and/or internal-consistency failure where the agent flags the template's own sanctioned idiom. The under-finding FAILs (contracts 0.50, data-lineage 0.17) are FIXTURE-grading defects, not prompt weakness. The observability cluster (observability, -db, -fe, -infra) shares one mechanism: flagging the ABSENCE of MANUAL instrumentation the template AUTO-provides. Fix = (1) prepend the shared rubric to every prompt; (2) add agent-specific codebase-bar carve-outs naming the exact template idiom; (3) re-author the handful of invalid fixtures; (4) block only on 'high' everywhere, advisory agents never block.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Absence of MANUAL observability instrumentation (manual span / per-query metric / per-endpoint SLO / per-endpoint business metric / manual correlation_id) on a path the template AUTO-instruments
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: NOT A FINDING (drop entirely). The template auto-instruments at the engine/middleware/global level (SQLAlchemyInstrumentor, FastAPIInstrumentor, ObservabilityMiddleware, add_correlation_id/add_trace_context processors, window 'error'/'unhandledrejection' global handler, fleet-wide-only SLOs). The template's own routes/items.py, repository.create_item/list_items, App.tsx/Items.tsx carry NO manual instrumentation and ship unflagged. Per the codebase-bar principle, flagging a new path for lacking manual instrumentation holds it stricter than the codebase and would flag EVERY function in the repo (the Plan-18 over-flagging mechanism). Re-scope each observability agent to flag only a REAL gap relative to what the template provides: a path that BYPASSES auto-instrumentation (raw driver / second un-registered engine / background path), an ACTIVE suppression of the global signal (a catch that consumes a rejection so it never reaches the global handler / js-errors counter), a new mutation/business path that emits NO structured log event of its own, a new prod surface with no /health or no observability quartet (service+exporter+scrape+alert+dashboard).
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: Unanimous across the observability cohort once the codebase fact (auto-instrumentation) is applied. observability.json is the load-bearing case (its three explicit flag clauses each contradict the shipped template); -db/-fe/-infra are the same defect specialized.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Missing defense-in-depth hardening on a config secret (SecretStr vs str, min_length/entropy floor, rotation docs, .env handling)
agents_calling_high: security (r1 only — severity-unstable, same finding rated medium in r0/r2)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: INFO at most, never medium/high, and only if not already redundant with an existing repo mitigation (.env is already in template .gitignore). 'Secret-exposing = high' binds ONLY to an actual committed/leaked secret VALUE on a changed line (hardcoded default, key in code/config, secret in a logged field). The template's own webhook_signing_secret is a plain `str = ""` with no SecretStr and no length floor (SecretStr/get_secret_value/min_length: 0 hits in template/), so the good fixture's `api_secret_key: str` is byte-idiomatic and must score [].
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: security.json: the bad fixture (hardcoded default) is a clean critical 3/3; the good-fixture over-flag is 100% hardening-as-blocking + intra-agent severity instability (SecretStr high in r1, medium in r0/r2). The codebase-bar clause + binding 'secret-exposing' to a real value resolves both.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: PII in logs / PII echoed in responses / PII captured into telemetry (RUM allowlist)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: HIGH, but OWNED BY PRIVACY ONLY. privacy correctly rates these high and is the cleanest agent in the corpus (recall 1.00 / fp 0.00). compliance currently DUPLICATES privacy's PII-in-logs frame (2 of 3 findings per bad repeat, and the same frame drives compliance's good-fixture fp 1.00). Reconciliation: compliance must DEFER raw-PII-in-logs/PII-collection to privacy and stay on its own axis — AUDIT-GAP / RETENTION-PATH / ERASURE-PATH. A sensitive op with NO audit record, or persisted personal data with NO retention/deletion path, is compliance-high; a logger merely receiving an identifier is NOT.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: privacy.json + compliance.json. The same PII-in-logs phenomenon is correctly high in privacy and is scope-creep in compliance. Cross-agent boundary: privacy owns the PII SINK; compliance owns the AUDIT/RETENTION GAP.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Structured get_logger().info(event, key=value) with an OPAQUE identifier and no per-line retention policy
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: NOT A FINDING. This is the template's own sanctioned request-logging idiom (middleware/observability.py:59-65 logs every request this way; webhooks/inbox.py emits structured events the same way). An opaque-id structured log with no in-handler retention policy is clean. compliance's fp 1.00 is entirely this codebase-bar violation + hallucinated 'no log-retention policy declared in a route handler' over-reach.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: compliance.json codebase_bar_violations vs privacy.json stricter_than_codebase_note (privacy verified the same middleware logs only method/path/correlation_id and did NOT over-flag). The two agents must agree: opaque-id structured logging is clean.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Missing post-commit session.refresh() / 'created_at=None' / absent UNIQUE constraint on a bulk insert
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: NOT A FINDING. The session factory sets expire_on_commit=False (ORM attributes survive commit without refresh), created_at uses server_default=func.now() (populated in the DB regardless), and items.name has NO UNIQUE constraint in the template (migration 0001 = NOT NULL only). data-integrity's fp 1.00 is a hallucinated 'created_at=None' high + a stricter-than-codebase UNIQUE demand + an internal-consistency failure (it demands validation on the bad fixture then flags the good fixture HIGH for HAVING that same validation). HIGH stays reserved for the genuine planted defect: the in-loop session.commit() that breaks batch atomicity.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: data-integrity.json. The create_item/bulk-insert internal-consistency trap the rubric names by reference.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Bounded full-ORM-row over-fetch (hydrate all columns, consume one) when the result set is capped
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: NOT A FINDING. The template's own /items endpoint hydrates created_at and discards it on every request, unflagged, bounded by MAX_PAGE_SIZE. Bounded reads are not performance defects; reserve findings for UNBOUNDED scans, N+1 round-trips, or super-linear work on unbounded input. performance's bad-fixture detection (N+1 + unbounded scan, high 3/3) is excellent; only the good-fixture low-noise needs to drop to [].
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: performance.json. Same one-standard-per-pattern lesson: the agent suggests projection on the bad fixture then penalizes bounded full-row fetch on the good fixture, while the template ships full-row fetch as the norm.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Absence of casing/whitespace normalization (strip/lower) on a stored value
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: NOT A LINEAGE FINDING. The template's create_item stores name verbatim, unflagged; flagging absence of normalization is both stricter-than-codebase AND out of the lineage domain (no PII destination, no deletion gap, no cross-paradigm write). Reviewers correctly returned []. data-lineage recall 0.17 is caused entirely by this one INVALID bad fixture (untransformed-write); fix is to REPLACE the fixture, not weaken the prompt.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: data-lineage.json. The recall FAIL is a fixture defect; the prompt is fundamentally correct.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Optional undeclared read (.get(...)) / stale-but-non-breaking pact / additive backwards-compatible change
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: INFO/LOW, never high. An OPTIONAL undeclared read (data.get('location')) or a not-yet-regenerated pact is advisory; a HARD undeclared dependency (int(data['field']) -> KeyError) is high. contracts recall 0.50 is a FIXTURE-grading bug: bad/client-pact-divergence exercises only info-tier behavior but is graded against block_threshold='high'. All 3 reviewers correctly returned info. Fix the fixture (convert to a hard undeclared dependency), not the prompt or threshold.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: contracts.json + api-design.json agree: additive/optional = non-blocking; hard/breaking = high. The reviewers were RIGHT; the fixture is mis-filed.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: A var wired only into base.yml (dev/test overlay) but needed in prod; container-reach of an env var
agents_calling_low_or_clean: env-parity (r1 abstained — incorrectly, on a host-env-reaches-app assumption)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: HIGH (it is a real parity gap — prod.yml/staging.yml have no env_file passthrough, so a base.yml-only var never reaches the deployed app). The non-determinism is a fixture+prompt seam, not over-flagging: the 'good' fixture parity-preserved is byte-identical in the compose hunk to the bad fixture, distinguished only by a parity-IRRELEVANT .env.example line. Fix = re-author the good fixture to a pure consumed-and-declared env-var change (drop the base.yml hunk) AND add a prompt clause stating the container-reach rule explicitly.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: env-parity.json. fp 0.67 is one mis-authored good fixture on an unresolved container-reach seam; recall is a clean 1.00.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Latent/speculative-future correctness fragility (works today, breaks under a hypothetical future change)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: MEDIUM, never escalated to high. A correctness defect real TODAY (wrong output, data loss, crash, swallowed error on the changed path) = high; a latent fragility relying on accidental correctness = medium. application-logic's reviewers correctly held the falsy-check finding at medium across 3 repeats — but that was model luck (the prompt has no severity anchor). Inherit the rubric to make it stable.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: application-logic.json. Also flag: the bad fixture ships a FACTUALLY WRONG inline comment ('returns 404 for any id=0') that could induce a hallucinated high; fix the comment.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Redundant/duplicate test coverage (verbatim duplicate of an existing parametrized case)
agents_calling_low_or_clean: test-quality (r1 low, r2 medium — same finding, three severities)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: LOW/INFO advisory only, NEVER high or blocking. HIGH for test-quality is reserved strictly for 'a test that cannot fail' (tautology, mock of the symbol-under-test, interface-mismatch mock, unhappy-path-without-failure-assert). test-quality's fp 1.00 is a genuinely-redundant test in the good fixture (visible via the bundle context) + an unanchored prompt that rated the SAME duplicate high/low/medium across repeats. Fix BOTH: replace the duplicate test in the good fixture; anchor redundancy to low.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: test-quality.json. Recall is perfect (1.0 high stable); the fp is a coupled fixture+prompt defect.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Advisory UX observations on clean frontend code (confirm-dialog ergonomics, success toast, window.confirm caveats, component-not-yet-wired)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: INFO (advisory) — agent NEVER blocks. usability fp 0.33 is a CALIBRATION ARTIFACT: thresholds.yaml holds it to fp_max 0.10 like a blocking agent while block_threshold=None and the scorer counts any surfaced info as a positive. Realign to advisory semantics (fp_max 1.00, mirroring dependency/documentation). Allow 'low' for concrete defects (silent mutation failure, no error branch) and keep 'info' for taste. Add a scope exclusion: a new component not yet wired into a parent is NOT a usability defect.
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: usability.json. Same advisory-calibration class as dependency and documentation.

cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.class: Dependency-manifest convention violation (unpinned dep when every sibling is pinned)
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.reconciled_bar: LOW/INFO advisory — agent NEVER blocks (fp_max 1.00 by design). Under the bare rubric an unpinned dep is a 'medium' convention violation, so dependency must EXPLICITLY opt out of the medium tier ('this advisory agent caps at low even for convention violations'). Reserve a single narrow optional 'high' ONLY for a concrete supply-chain compromise (malicious/typosquat/yanked) and keep even that advisory until a fixture proves high-precision detection. Cut fabricated CVE-specific claims and out-of-domain async/event-loop reasoning (performance's territory).
cross_agent_severity_reconciliation.rationale: dependency.json. Threshold-PASS but qualitatively noisy: scope-creep into runtime reasoning + fabricated CVEs + a good fixture that is not truly clean (httpx>=0.27 diverges from the template's >=0.28).

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: PII sink: PII logged, echoed in responses, captured into telemetry/RUM, unnecessary collection, retention-beyond-purpose
scope_boundary_enforcement.defers_to_others: audit/retention GAP -> compliance; authz -> security; observability coverage -> observability

scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: raw PII-in-logs (privacy), name_filter discarded / pagination (application-logic / api-design)

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: structured-log presence on a new mutation/business path; swallowed error branch
scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: DB rollback/session state (data-integrity), idempotency/business correctness (application-logic), import-order/exc_info style (none/code-quality)

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: DB access bypassing auto-instrumentation; new client with no /health; data-layer error without correlation_id
active suppression of the global error signal; unbounded label cardinality

scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: runtime async/event-loop behavior the manifest diff cannot show (performance / application-logic)

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: missing docstring on a documented sibling, missing .env.example var, stale README/spec entry
scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: page-size cap correctness, response_model/typing, openapi.json regen, CI contract jobs (api-design / contracts / performance)

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: layering violation, circular dep, coupling, heavy inline handler
actual committed/leaked secret value, exploitable vuln
unbounded scans, N+1, super-linear-on-unbounded, SLO regression
scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: bounded micro-optimisation matching the repo idiom

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: user-observable harm: blank/frozen/silent-failure UI, unrecoverable misclick
scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: feature-wiring completeness, implementation taste (reload vs state update)

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: correctness: edge cases, wrong conditionals, missing/swallowed error handling
provider break of a committed pact, uncompensated incompatible change, weakened consumer assertion
non-atomic writes, constraint/migration data loss, broken store invariants
scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: missing refresh (expire_on_commit=False), absent UNIQUE the schema never declared

scope_boundary_enforcement.owns: PII to undocumented store, deletion gap missing a store, stale documented/indexed derived field
service/env-var parity across deploy targets, container-reach
scope_boundary_enforcement.must_stop_flagging: config VALUE divergence, observability, PII/secret content

refined_shared_rubric.preamble: Every review-* agent prompt MUST prepend this rubric verbatim. The audit confirmed that EVERY good-fixture over-flag in the corpus traces to an agent NOT inheriting these clauses. This refined version adds: (a) an auto-instrumentation codebase-bar example (the observability cohort's root cause), (b) an explicit advisory-tier opt-out for never-blocking agents, and (c) a grounding clause (cite only facts you READ this run) prompted by env-parity/observability-infra hallucinations.

severity.high: blocks a builder: a concrete, demonstrable defect that WILL cause incorrect behavior, data loss, a security/privacy breach, or a broken contract in the CHANGED code. A latent fragility that only breaks under a hypothetical future change is NOT high.
severity.medium: should fix before merge but does not block: a real issue with a plausible path to harm, or a clear violation of an established project convention.
severity.low: advisory: style, minor clarity, redundant test coverage, bounded micro-optimisation, or a non-urgent improvement.
severity.info: observation only; never implies an action is required.

refined_shared_rubric.codebase_bar_principle: Do not hold new code to a stricter standard than the surrounding codebase already meets. Before flagging a pattern, check whether the template/baseline already does the same thing unflagged; if it does, do not flag the new instance. SPECIFIC, BINDING EXAMPLES (verified against the template): (1) data stores are AUTO-instrumented at the engine/middleware/global level (SQLAlchemyInstrumentor, FastAPIInstrumentor, ObservabilityMiddleware, add_correlation_id/add_trace_context, window 'error'/'unhandledrejection' handler) — a plain path that runs through them is ALREADY observed; do NOT flag absence of a manual span/metric/correlation_id. (2) SLOs are fleet-wide only; there is NO per-endpoint SLO convention — do not demand one. (3) the session factory sets expire_on_commit=False and created_at uses server_default — a missing post-commit refresh is NOT a defect. (4) items.name has no UNIQUE constraint — absence of de-dup is not a violated invariant. (5) the template's signing secret is a plain str with no SecretStr/min_length — those are info hardening, never blocking. (6) bounded reads (capped by MAX_PAGE_SIZE) hydrating full ORM rows are the repo norm — not a performance defect. (7) repository.create_item stores values verbatim (no normalization).
refined_shared_rubric.internal_consistency: Apply one standard to every instance of a pattern you see in the same diff. If you do not flag instance A, do not flag an identical instance B (the create_item/bulk-insert lesson). Do not demand a mitigation on one fixture then flag a sibling fixture for HAVING that same mitigation.
refined_shared_rubric.scope_discipline: Stay within this agent's domain (stated per-agent, with explicit 'do NOT flag X — agent Y owns it' carve-outs). Do not flag issues another agent owns.
refined_shared_rubric.grounding: Cite only file/line facts you actually READ in THIS run. Do not enumerate .env.example / settings / file contents from memory, and do not assert what is or isn't declared without a read. Treat files CREATED or MODIFIED in this diff as present — do not flag a surface as missing an alert/dashboard/declaration that the same change adds. Speculative 'IF the middleware does not...' findings against established wiring are disallowed.
refined_shared_rubric.advisory_tier_optout: Advisory agents (usability, dependency, documentation) NEVER block (block_threshold=None) and are calibrated fp_max=1.00. They cap output at low/info even for a clear convention violation, and must explicitly opt out of the 'medium' tier. They surface observations by design; surfacing an info finding on clean code is NOT a false positive for them.
refined_shared_rubric.output: Return JSON ONLY — an array of {"path","line","severity","message","suggestion"}; output exactly [] if none. No prose, no code fence, no commentary before or after the array.

block_threshold_consolidation.block_on_high_advisory_none: Every blocking agent blocks ONLY on 'high'; medium/low/info never block. Advisory agents (usability, dependency, documentation) have block_threshold=None and are calibrated fp_max=1.00.

per_agent.compliance: high (advisory until post-retune given current fp 1.00)

replace_invalid_bad.action: REPLACE with an in-domain bad (deletion-gap-misses-a-store OR pii-to-undocumented-sink) — the absence-of-normalization defect is stricter-than-codebase AND out of domain; it single-handedly drives recall to 0.17
REPLACE with raw-connection-bypasses-instrumentation (a second un-registered engine/raw DBAPI call); current fixture is byte-identical body to the good fixture
REPLACE with new-client-no-health OR db-error-no-correlation-id; current fixture's 'invisible to observability' docstring is factually false (auto-instrumented)

fix_invalid_good.action: Re-author to a pure consumed+declared env-var change; drop the base.yml-only hunk (currently byte-identical compose hunk to the bad fixture, a real parity gap)
Make genuinely clean — device_class is stripped by the fail-closed allowlist (dead-on-arrival). Add device_class to the allowlist in-patch OR use an allowlisted/server-derived dimension
Repair the dev.yml hunk so the redis: block lands under services:, not volumes: (currently parses as a malformed volume)
Remove the doc-vs-impl mismatch — count_items returns a bounded page (<=DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE) while docs promise a true total; implement a real aggregate OR reword to honest bounded semantics
Replace the verbatim-duplicate test (test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info duplicates the existing parametrized [prod] case) with a genuinely additive test
Change httpx>=0.27,<1.0 to httpx>=0.28 (match the template floor, drop the non-template cap) so it is truly clean

regrade_or_convert_bad.action: CONVERT the optional read into a HARD undeclared dependency (int(data['field']) -> KeyError with no pact declaration) so the info-tier case becomes a genuine high; also ADD bad/weakened-consumer-assertion for the untested third high rule
Re-author to a true compliance defect (sensitive op with NO audit record OR persisted PII with no retention path); move email-in-logs to the privacy corpus

fix_misleading_content.action: Correct the factually-wrong comment ('returns 404 for any id=0' is false — item is the ORM object, not item_id); state the real latent-ORM-truthiness defect
RENAME to graphql-breaking-field-rename (defect is valid; name mis-advertises a REST-parity responsibility the prompt does not own)
Regenerate change.patch as a full git-formatted diff (add diff --git/index header) for stable line anchoring; do NOT change the defect

add_coverage.action: Add good+bad pairs for the untested high categories: non-semantic-interactive (div-onClick) and keyboard-inaccessibility; only img-alt is currently exercised
Add bad/heavy-inline-handler + good/lightweight-inline-handler to cover the prompt's untested second domain and pin the heavy-vs-lightweight boundary
Add good/graphql-additive-field (additive optional GraphQL field returns []) — the real over-flagging boundary; current good fixture is REST-only (scope-obedience, not GraphQL judgment)
Optional: add a 2nd good fixture (pii-redacted-log) to widen fp coverage (only 1 good case today)

prompt_actions_summary: ALL 20 agents: prepend the refined shared rubric. Agent-specific load-bearing clauses: observability/-db/-fe/-infra = auto-instrumentation codebase-bar + re-scope to bypass/active-suppression/missing-quartet (observability.json is the highest-priority rewrite); compliance = narrow to audit/retention/erasure + defer PII to privacy + opaque-id-log carve-out; security = bind 'secret-exposing' to a real committed value + hardening=info; data-integrity = expire_on_commit/server_default/no-UNIQUE carve-out + demonstrability gate replacing the 'at least high' floor; performance = bounded-reads-are-not-defects clause; dependency = scope-discipline (no runtime reasoning) + no-fabricated-CVE + explicit advisory cap; documentation = scope fence + cap at low/info; usability = scope exclusion (wiring completeness) + allow low for concrete defects + JSON-only hardening; data-lineage = add derived-field-desync class pinned high + normalization carve-out; contracts = hard-vs-optional-dependency clarifier (keep existing correct info classifications); application-logic = high/medium correctness-calibration line; env-parity = container-reach rule + grounding clause; accessibility/architecture/api-design/privacy = rubric inheritance + minor scope/severity lines (these are already PASS).
threshold_notes: Do NOT widen any fp_max to 'absorb' a current over-flag — every elevated fp in this baseline is a prompt/fixture defect, not real over-flagging; widening would mask future regressions. Re-derive all numeric thresholds in Phase 3 (eval-analyze) from a post-fix --repeat sweep. Advisory agents (usability, dependency, documentation): fp_max=1.00, recall_min~0.90. observability-db: defer threshold entirely until the coupled prompt+fixture redesign produces a true-positive signal.
notes: Reconciliation is strongly convergent: 18 of 20 agents share one root cause (no rubric inheritance -> codebase-bar/internal-consistency over-flags), and the 2 FAIL-on-recall agents (contracts 0.50, data-lineage 0.17) are unanimous FIXTURE-grading defects where the reviewers behaved correctly. No agent's prompt needs to be made STRICTER; every fix loosens an over-flag or repairs a fixture. The observability cohort is the single highest-leverage cluster (4 agents, one mechanism).
